Title: FW: viaautofinance Completed: Agreement confirmation, Pending eSign Review On Docx_fbe926031a7f2d2e719c991f2af87b92f04898ea 1/15/2026 REF. ID:5498968406
Notes: Mike reported being unable to block a specific email. Two options were provided: a Teams call for live assistance or step-by-step instructions with screenshots. The client chose a Teams call. During the call, guidance was provided on blocking emails in Outlook. The main issue was that the sender address appeared grayed out due to being a large spam address, preventing the hyperlink from displaying. It was explained that this behavior can occur with certain spam messages, and the issue will be further reviewed and troubleshooted after hours.

Title: FW: viaautofinance Completed: Agreement confirmation, Pending eSign Review On Docx_fbe926031a7f2d2e719c991f2af87b92f04898ea 1/15/2026 REF. ID:5498968406
Notes:
Ticket Summary – Email Hyperlink Issue (Resolved)

Mike reported that an email received from an external sender did not display a clickable hyperlink. Investigation confirmed the message was flagged as phishing by myCleanMail due to malformed sender headers and suspicious formatting from the vendor’s email system. As part of the security action, the email was sanitized and converted to plain text, which removed the hyperlink.

When the message was forwarded internally, Outlook re-rendered it as HTML, which is why the hyperlink became visible to other recipients.

I blocked the domain.

Title: Disk space issue on VAF-DC-TSVDI-GI
Notes: As per the alert connected to VAF-DC-TSVDI-GI download and install TreeSize, a disk space management tool that provides a detailed overview of the disk usage across folders and files on the system. After installation, TreeSize was run to analyse the disk. TreeSize scans the system and generates a report showing the size of all folders and files in a hierarchical structure so carefully review all folders and files occupying excessive space. Based on the review, we proceeded to clear unnecessary files that were identified as no longer needed. This included: Temporary Files: Files in the system's temporary folder are often created by applications during installation, updates, or other processes; clear these temporary files to free up space. Recycle Bin: emptied the Recycle Bin, where deleted files are temporarily stored. This ensured that deleted files were permanently removed from the disk and no longer took up space. Downloaded Items: System error memory dump files, Old Windows updates, previous Windows installations other system cache files. Now Sufficient Free Space Available.

Title: unable to connect to sftp.viaautofinance.com
Notes:
As per handover from Ajay its was still not working so troubleshooting the issue but had no luck so took the backup of the VM from HQ and uploaded to the Onedrive and download at DC and restored the VM at DC with VAF network and folder and assigned the tag and configured the IP address and verified internet is working fine and then ping and nslookup the url sftp.viaautofinance.com and its still directed to HQ IP address so tried to login into the DNS provider portal and then changed the routing to HQ IP to DC and then tried to ping and its giving the correct the information The issue started when the SFTP connection to sftp.viaautofinance.com failed, with the error message stating, "The server rejected SFTP connection, but it listens for FTP connections." Initially, DNS resolution was verified, and the domain was resolving correctly to the public IP (66.81.19.90). The Windows Firewall rule for port 22 was confirmed to be active, allowing inbound SFTP traffic. I also ensured that Cerberus FTP Server was configured correctly to listen for SFTP connections on port 22, and the SFTP listener was active in the server settings.
To further investigate, I ran the telnet command to verify the connection on port 22, which failed, confirming that the issue was not isolated to a specific client. Additionally, I tested connectivity via WinSCP, but the connection attempt failed with the same error. After reviewing the Cerberus FTP Server configuration, I found that the trial license had expired, which likely caused the server to stop accepting SFTP connections.

The NAT rule on the Meraki firewall and the internal firewall settings were correctly forwarding traffic from the public IP to the internal server IP on port 22, which ruled out any issues with the network configuration. However, it was clear that the expired Cerberus FTP Server trial license was the root cause, as it likely disabled SFTP functionality. The next step will be to apply a commercial license or extend the trial to restore full SFTP functionality.

Title: unable to connect to sftp.viaautofinance.com
Notes: The issue was initially suspected to be related to the expired Cerberus FTP Server trial license, so I opened a case with the support team to request a trial key extension. While awaiting their response, I continued troubleshooting and decided to create a new VM identical to the current setup, ensuring all configurations were replicated. I installed Cerberus FTP Server on the new VM, completed the necessary configurations, and created the required user accounts. After setting everything up, I tested the server and confirmed that SFTP connections were successfully established and everything was working fine. In parallel, I received the trial key from the support team, which is valid for 15 days, and applied it to the new VM, restoring full functionality.

Title: Install Technijian Tools on VAF-HQ-LT-33 and  VAF-HQ-LT-35.
Notes:
Request: Mike requested the installation of technician tools on two laptops, including VAF-HQ-LT-33.

Issue Encountered:

Technician tools were successfully installed on VAF-HQ-LT-33., but while configuring MyRemote for access, an error appeared related to admin policy.

Upon investigation in the Group Policies and Registry Editor, no policy was found blocking access.

Troubleshooting:

After further troubleshooting, it was discovered that Windows Defender was blocking the MyRemote app from gaining access.

Solution: I disabled Windows Defender, which resolved the issue and allowed the MyRemote app to function correctly.

Outcome:

After turning off Defender, MyRemote was successfully installed and configured for access.

I provided Mike with access to MyRemote on the affected laptop.
